retrieved_result: 6.6 Materials for Flywheels From Materials Selection in Mechanical Design, Third Edition6.6 Materials for Flywheels Flywheels store energy. Small ones the sort found in children's toys are made of lead. Old steam engines have flywheels; they are made of cast iron. Cars have them too (though you cannot see them) to smooth power
